item.title: 马科斯·丁纳斯坦 | Here Comes PvE Season Three! By Marcos Dinnerstein
item.description:
密码束

发表于在我们开始之前 - 您可以通过下载 MetaMask 或 ioPay 钱包，然后访问 StarCrazy.com，立即开始玩 StarCrazy

进入游戏步骤：

第 1 步：安装元掩码： https ://metamask.io/

第二步：添加自定义RPC/Network在MetaMask浏览器扩展中，打开顶部的“Networks”下拉菜单，点击“Add Network”：

第 3 步：输入 IoTeX 主网的相应网络值： 网络名称：“IoTeX 主网”

链号：4689

符号：IOTXExplorer 网址： https ://iotexscan.io

第 4 步：单击保存按钮并确保选择 IoTeX 作为活动网络

第五步：点击链接进入游戏： https ://game.starcrazy.com/?cid=nov

我们距离新的 PvE 赛季，即第 3 赛季只有几天的时间。这个赛季将于 11 月 10 日开始，持续两个月。 以下是您需要了解的有关即将到来的季节的信息。

  • 奖励将与上一季相同，但本季不会有纪念 NFT。 第 3 赛季的 PvE 中的 Boss 将与第 1 赛季相同，即有两名学徒和一名法师。

我们听说您担心使用您的行星和矿石。 当然，这是一个可以理解的担忧。 您希望获得重大投资的回报。

我们认为以下解释应该会让您放心。 星际矿山产出的矿石，未来将通过以下方式产生收益。

您将产生或促进学习或获得技能。

行星所有者，您可以通过让玩家

  1. 鉴定技能书
  2. 合成一本技能书
  3. 让 Starz 学习
  4. 生成新的技能书

所有上述费用都需要以 GFT 的形式支付给星球所有者。

在寄售商店中，您可以与 GFT 交易矿石和技能书。 只有未绑定的物品才能在商店中交易。 可以建造的建筑物数量取决于地球的大小。 以下是每种大小的行星可以建造的数量：N 1、超大 2、超大 3。

你的建筑的最高质量不能超过你星球的稀有等级。 首次建造建筑物时，建筑物质量是随机的。

您可以使用蓝图来提升建筑物的质量

矿石的数量取决于行星的大小。

采矿率和你的运气取决于你的建筑质量。 您可以通过升级建筑物来提高采矿率和运气。

事情变得越来越复杂，但以一种好的方式。 这意味着我们将有更多有趣的事情要做，也有更多的赚钱方式。 我们喜欢那样！建一座大楼。

您可以使用蓝图来提升建筑物的质量

矿石的数量取决于行星的大小。

采矿率和你的运气取决于你的建筑质量。 您可以通过升级建筑物来提高采矿率和运气。

事情变得越来越复杂，但以一种好的方式。 这意味着我们将有更多有趣的事情要做，也有更多的赚钱方式。 我们喜欢这样！

进入游戏步骤：

第 1 步：安装元掩码： https ://metamask.io/

第二步：添加自定义RPC/Network在MetaMask浏览器扩展中，打开顶部的“Networks”下拉菜单，点击“Add Network”：

第 3 步：输入 IoTeX 主网的相应网络值： 网络名称：“IoTeX 主网”

链号：4689

符号：IOTXExplorer 网址： https ://iotexscan.io

第 4 步：单击保存按钮并确保选择 IoTeX 作为活动网络

第五步：点击链接进入游戏： https ://game.starcrazy.com/?cid=nov

开个玩笑，这是个人喜好。 但是你可以在你的设置中更改你的主题、字体等。

你知道的越多。 一旦暂停，crypto_gal01 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，crypto_gal01 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，crypto_gal01 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 crypto_gal01 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 crypto_gal01 的方法：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 crypto_gal01 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

crypto_bunch

Posted on Nov 16

Before we get rolling - You can start playing StarCrazy today by downloading either MetaMask or ioPay wallets and then going to StarCrazy.com

Steps to enter the game:

Step 1: Install the metamask: https://metamask.io/

Step 2: Add a custom RPC/NetworkIn the MetaMask browser extension, open the "Networks" drop-down menu at the top, and click the "Add Network":

Step 3: Input the respective network values for IoTeX Mainnet: Network Name: "IoTeX Mainnet"

RPC URL: See Babel API Endpoints » (Example: https://babel-api.mainnet.iotex.io)

Chain ID: 4689

Symbol: IOTXExplorer URL: https://iotexscan.io

Step 4: Click the Save button and make sure IoTeX is selected as the active network

Step 5: Click the link to enter the game: https://game.starcrazy.com/?cid=nov

We’re just days away from a new PvE season, Season 3. This season will begin on November 10th and will last for two months. Here’s what you need to know about the coming season.

  • The rewards will be the same as they were for last season but there will be no memorial NFTs this season. (Sorry! If we gave them every season they wouldn’t be as special, would they?)

  • For Season 3 the Boss in PvE will be the same as Season 1, that is, there will be two Apprentices and one Wizard.

We hear you are concerned about using your planets and ores. Of course this is an understandable concern. You want a return on your significant investment.

We think the following explanation should set your minds at ease. Ores produced in the interstellar mines will be used in the future to generate income in the following ways.

You will be producing or facilitating the learning or acquisition of a skill.

Planets owners, you’ll be able to earn by letting players

  1. Authenticate a skill book
  2. Synthesize a skill book
  3. Allow a Starz to learn
  4. Generate new skill books

For all the above fees will need to be paid in GFT to the planet owner.

In the Consignment Store you can trade ores and skill books with GFT. Only unbound items can be traded in the store. (you’ll see a locked icon in the top-right corner, if it is bound. If no lock is visible then it’s not bound)

*More About Construction on Planets
*
The number of buildings that can be built depends on the size of the planet. Here’s how many can be built for each size planet: N 1, Huge 2, Super Huge 3.

The highest quality of your building can not exceed your planet’s rarity rating. (N, R, SSR, etc.)

Building quality is random when first building a building.

You can use blueprints to upgrade the quality of your building

The amount of ores depends on the planet’s size.

The mining rate and your luck depend on the quality of your building. You can increase both mining rate and luck by upgrading your building.

Rare ores needed for Starz to learn skills and upgrade your buildings.

Things are getting more complicated but in a good way. That means we’ll have more interesting things to do and more ways to earn. We like that!building a building.

You can use blueprints to upgrade the quality of your building

The amount of ores depends on the planet’s size.

The mining rate and your luck depend on the quality of your building. You can increase both mining rate and luck by upgrading your building.

Rare ores needed for Starz to learn skills and upgrade your buildings.

Things are getting more complicated but in a good way. That means we’ll have more interesting things to do and more ways to earn. We like that!

Steps to enter the game:

Step 1: Install the metamask: https://metamask.io/

Step 2: Add a custom RPC/NetworkIn the MetaMask browser extension, open the "Networks" drop-down menu at the top, and click the "Add Network":

Step 3: Input the respective network values for IoTeX Mainnet: Network Name: "IoTeX Mainnet"

RPC URL: See Babel API Endpoints » (Example: https://babel-api.mainnet.iotex.io)

Chain ID: 4689

Symbol: IOTXExplorer URL: https://iotexscan.io

Step 4: Click the Save button and make sure IoTeX is selected as the active network

Step 5: Click the link to enter the game: https://game.starcrazy.com/?cid=nov

Just kidding, it's a personal preference. But you can change your theme, font, etc. in your settings.

The more you know. 🌈

Once suspended, crypto_gal01 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, crypto_gal01 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by crypto_gal01 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If crypto_gal01 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag crypto_gal01:

crypto_gal01 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging crypto_gal01 will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: crypto_gal01

item.title: 使用 Typescript 构建你的第一个 Web3 Chrome 扩展 | Build your first Web3 Chrome Extension using Typescript
item.description:
今天，我将引导您完成构建一个简单的web3 chrome 扩展程序的步骤，该扩展程序可以获取给定钱包地址的 ETH 余额。

在本教程中，您将学习以下内容：

  • 设置 Webpack 开发环境。
  • 进行 Web3 调用。
  • 创建 Chrome 扩展程序并了解 Manifest。

我假设您具有基本的 Javascript 知识，所以我不会过多地介绍代码结构。

在我们开始之前，请确保您的计算机上安装了Nodejs和NPM 。

让我们立即开始并开始设置我们的环境。


我们从初始化项目和创建package.json文件开始

mkdir ts-web3-extension && cd ts-web3-extension && npm init -ydi

打字稿：

它是建立在它之上的Javascript超集。
与动态类型的 Javascript 不同，Typescript 提供可选的静态类型和额外功能，可帮助您识别常见错误并编写更简洁的代码。

您可以将 Typescript 安装到您的项目中或在您的计算机上全局安装。

 npm i typescript --save-dev

网页包：

要开始使用 Webpack 构建我们的开发设置，我们需要安装一些依赖项：

 npm i webpack webpack-cli html-webpack-plugin clean-webpack-plugin --save-dev

默认情况下，webpack 只能编译和打包Javascript 文件，所以我们需要使用一个加载器来处理 Typescript 文件。

将下面的命令复制到您的终端以安装 ts-loader

 npm i ts-loader --save-dev

现在我们已经安装了所有需要的依赖项，让我们开始构建我们的环境。

我们在根目录下创建tsconfig.ts文件

让我们暂时将文件留空，或者只添加{} ，这样我们就可以使用编译器的默认值。

接下来，我们创建webpack.config.js

现在，将下面的代码复制到其中

const HTMLWebpackPlugin = require('html-webpack-plugin'); const { CleanWebpackPlugin } = require('clean-webpack-plugin'); const path = require('path'); module.exports = { mode: 'development', entry: './src/index.ts', output: { path: path.resolve(__dirname, './dist'), filename: 'index.js' }, devtool: 'cheap-module-source-map', module: { rules: [ { test: /\.ts$/, exclude: /node_modules/, use: { loader: 'ts-loader', }, } ], }, plugins: [ new HTMLWebpackPlugin(), new CleanWebpackPlugin(), ] };

那么，让我们分别解释每个配置选项

如果将其更改为production环境，您会注意到构建是如何缩小和压缩的。

之后，我们将devtool设置为​​cheap-module-source-map 。
默认情况下，devtool 在其源映射样式中使用eval函数，这是一种不好的做法，chrome 不允许使用它，因此这将删除它。

在module中，我们指定了一组规则，在这里我们确定 webpack 如何使用ts-loader处理 Typescript 文件。

我们设置了一个正则表达式来test运行 ts-loader 的文件结尾，并且在exclude中我们告诉 webpack 不要遍历node_modules依赖项。

与在文件被捆绑之前应用更改的加载程序不同， plugins在整个生成的捆绑包上工作并控制它们的创建方式和形式。

在这里我们添加了几个插件， html-webpack-plugin生成一个 HTML 文件，其中包含我们使用脚本标签的 webpack 包，无需手动创建它。 每次成功重建后clean-webpack-plugin会简单地删除构建目录中的所有文件。

好的，让我们进入主要部分并开始构建我们的应用程序

我们创建我们在 webpack 条目中指定的src目录，并在其中创建index.ts ，它将包含我们所有的代码。

 mkdir src && cd src && touch index.ts

将以下代码复制到 index.ts 中

let appEl = document.createElement('div'); appEl.setAttribute('id', 'app') document.body.appendChild(appEl); let app = document.getElementById('app')!; let balanceEl = document.createElement('h2'); balanceEl.setAttribute("id", "balance"); balanceEl.innerText = "0 ETH"; app.appendChild(balanceEl); let inputEl = document.createElement('input'); inputEl.setAttribute('type', 'text'); inputEl.setAttribute('id', 'address-input'); inputEl.setAttribute('name', 'address-input'); inputEl.setAttribute('placeholder', "Wallet Address'); app.appendChild(inputEl); let submitEl = document.createElement('button'); submitEl.setAttribute('id', 'submit-btn'); submitEl.innerText = 'Get Balance'; app.appendChild(submitEl);

在这里，我们为应用程序创建界面，只是一个简单的输入、一个按钮和一个文本元素。

请注意 document.getElementById('app') 后跟感叹号(!)的方式。 不确定它是否会返回非 null 或 undefined 的内容，因此它会抛出错误，并且通过添加非 null 断言运算符，我们声明该元素必须返回实际有意义的内容。

现在我们构建功能

让我们从安装 web3 依赖项开始

我们将 web3 导入 index.ts

 const Web3 = require('web3');

然后我们添加如下函数

const getBalance = async () => { submitEl.innerText = "Loading..."; submitEl.style.pointerEvents = 'none'; const infura = 'https://goerli.infura.io/v3/your-api-key'; const web3 = new Web3(new Web3.providers.HttpProvider(infura)); let addressValue = (document.getElementById('address-input') as HTMLFormElement).value; let balance = await web3.eth.getBalance(addressValue); balance = web3.utils.fromWei(balance, 'ether'); balanceEl.innerText = balance + " ETH"; submitEl.innerText = "Get Balance"; submitEl.style.pointerEvents = "auto"; };

默认情况下，Typescript 将异步函数的返回类型设置为Promise<type> ，因为它保证它会返回一个 promise，并且由于我们的函数通常没有返回值，因此它将类型设置为void 。

正如我们之前提到的，Typescript 强制我们显式显示，因此在 addressValue 中，我们将document.getElementById('address-input')设置为HTMLFormElement只是为了确保此输入元素具有实际值。

要连接到以太坊节点，我们将一个提供者传递到 web3 构造函数中，这里我们传递一个HttpProvider ，但如果节点在本地运行，您也可以使用允许订阅的WebSocketProvider和IpcProvider 。

然后，在您创建 API 密钥并将其直接传递给提供者之后，我们会从Infura获取我们的网络端点。

在这里，我们使用fromWei实用程序将我们获得的余额转换为可读的以太值。

完整代码：

 const Web3 = require('web3'); let appEl = document.createElement('div'); appEl.setAttribute('id', 'app') document.body.appendChild(appEl); let app = document.getElementById('app')!; let balanceEl = document.createElement('h2'); balanceEl.setAttribute("id", "balance"); balanceEl.innerText = "0 ETH"; app.appendChild(balanceEl); let inputEl = document.createElement('input'); inputEl.setAttribute("type", "text"); inputEl.setAttribute("id", "address-input"); inputEl.setAttribute("name", "address-input"); inputEl.setAttribute("placeholder", "Wallet Address"); app.appendChild(inputEl); let submitEl = document.createElement('button'); submitEl.setAttribute("id", "submit-btn"); submitEl.innerText = "Get Balance"; app.appendChild(submitEl); const getBalance = async () => { submitEl.innerText = "Loading..."; submitEl.style.pointerEvents = 'none'; const infura = 'https://goerli.infura.io/v3/9c29032f150b4868adf2b54794b3f0ec'; const web3 = new Web3(new Web3.providers.HttpProvider(infura)); let addressValue = (document.getElementById('address-input') as HTMLFormElement).value; let balance = await web3.eth.getBalance(addressValue); balance = web3.utils.fromWei(balance, 'ether'); balanceEl.innerText = balance + " ETH"; submitEl.innerText = "Get Balance"; submitEl.style.pointerEvents = "auto"; }; (document.getElementById("submit-btn") as HTMLFormElement).onclick = (e) => { e.preventDefault(); getBalance(); };

现在我们转向有趣的部分，即如何将所有这些变成 Chrome 扩展。

让我们在根目录中创建public并在其中创建manifest.json

 mkdir public && cd public && touch manifest.json

在manifest.json中，我们指定有关扩展的基本元数据和信息，例如名称、描述、chrome 当前支持的版本以及扩展功能的各个方面（例如操作）。

 { "name": "Web3Balance", "description": "Get ETH Balance of any given address", "version": "1.0", "manifest_version": 3, "action": { "default_popup": "index.html" } }

我们将默认弹出窗口设置为当用户单击扩展图标进入 webpack 将为我们生成的 html 文件时出现。

现在让我们安装一个额外的插件：

 npm i copy-webpack-plugin --save-dev

顾名思义，copy-webpack-plugin 将public中的manifest.json文件复制到dist文件夹中，以便清单文件能够访问生成的包。

现在让我们修改webpack.config.js并将插件添加到其中

const HTMLWebpackPlugin = require('html-webpack-plugin'); const CopyWebpackPlugin = require('copy-webpack-plugin'); const { CleanWebpackPlugin } = require('clean-webpack-plugin'); const path = require('path'); module.exports = { mode: 'development', entry: "./src/index.ts", output: { path: path.resolve(__dirname, './dist'), filename: 'index.js' }, devtool: "​​cheap-module-source-map", module: { rules: [ { test: /\.ts$/, exclude: /node_modules/, use: { loader: "ts-loader", }, } ], }, plugins: [ new HTMLWebpackPlugin(), new CopyWebpackPlugin({ patterns: [ {from: "public", to: "."} ], }), new CleanWebpackPlugin(), ] };

现在我们想为我们的应用程序添加一些样式，为此我们需要几个加载器

npm i css-loader style-loader --save-dev

让我们修改webpack.config.js

 const HTMLWebpackPlugin = require('html-webpack-plugin'); const CopyWebpackPlugin = require('copy-webpack-plugin'); const { CleanWebpackPlugin } = require('clean-webpack-plugin'); const path = require('path'); module.exports = { mode: 'development', entry: "./src/index.ts", output: { path: path.resolve(__dirname, './dist'), filename: 'index.js' }, devtool: "​​cheap-module-source-map", module: { rules: [ { test: /\.ts$/, exclude: /node_modules/, use: { loader: "ts-loader", }, }, { test: /\.css$/, exclude: /node_modules/, use: ["style-loader", "css-loader"], } ], }, plugins: [ new HTMLWebpackPlugin(), new CopyWebpackPlugin({ patterns: [ {from: "public", to: "."} ], }), new CleanWebpackPlugin(), ] };

在src目录下创建style.css ，将以下代码复制进去

#app{ width: 350px; padding: 20px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: center; } #balance{ margin: 0; } #address-input{ width: 100%; height: 30px; padding: 6px 15px; margin: 20px 0; border: none; outline: none; border-radius: 25px; background-color: #f3f3f3; } #submit-btn{ width: 50%; padding: 8px; cursor: pointer; border: none; border-radius: 8px; outline: none; }

现在我们将文件导入index.ts

最后，让我们将构建脚本添加到package.json中

"scripts": { "build": "webpack" }

现在我们在终端中运行以下命令

构建完成后，您会发现在包含生成的包的根目录中创建了dist文件夹。

现在让我们前往 Chrome 浏览器并导航至chrome://extensions

在页面的左上角点击Load unpacked来获取dist目录

等瞧！

完整代码可以在此处的 github 存储库中找到。

结论：

恭喜！ 你刚刚创建了你的第一个 Web3 Chrome 扩展！ 使用 Typescript，您可能会付出更多的努力来编写代码，但我相信这种努力是有回报的，而且所带来的好处是值得的。

我计划构建更复杂的项目，涵盖许多深入的 Web3 主题，所以请继续发布！



原文:

Today, I'm going to walk you through the steps of building a simple web3 chrome extension that gets the ETH balance of a given wallet address.

In this tutorial, you will learn the following:

  • Setting up a Webpack dev environment.
  • Some of Typescript core concepts.
  • Making a Web3 call.
  • Creating a Chrome Extension and learning about Manifest.

I assume you have basic Javascript knowledge, so I won't get much into the code structure.

Before we start, make sure you have Nodejs and NPM installed on your machine.

Let's jump right off and start setting up our environment.


We start with initializing our project and creating the package.json file

mkdir ts-web3-extension && cd ts-web3-extension && npm init -ydi

Typescript:

Typescript is a strongly typed language developed and maintained by Microsoft. It’s a superset of Javascript built up on it.
Unlike Javascript which is dynamically typed, Typescript provides optional static typing and extra features that help you identify common errors and write much cleaner code.

You can install Typescript into your project or globally on your machine.

npm i typescript --save-dev

Webpack:

Webpack is a module bundler for Javascript applications, it helps bundle and optimize different files we have in the app, making the code shorter and usable on the browser.

To start building our development setup using Webpack, we need to install some dependencies:

npm i webpack webpack-cli html-webpack-plugin clean-webpack-plugin --save-dev

By default, webpack can only compile and bundle Javascript files, so we will need to use a loader which will allow us to handle Typescript files.

Copy the command below into your terminal to install ts-loader

npm i ts-loader --save-dev

Now that we have installed all the dependencies needed, let's start building our environment.

We create tsconfig.ts file in the root directory

tsconfig.json indicates that this is the root of a TypeScript project.
Let's leave the file empty for now or just add {} which will let us use the compiler's defaults.

Next, we create webpack.config.js

Now, copy the code below into it

const HTMLWebpackPlugin = require('html-webpack-plugin');
const { CleanWebpackPlugin } = require('clean-webpack-plugin');
const path = require('path');

module.exports = {
  mode: 'development',
  entry: './src/index.ts',
  output: {
    path: path.resolve(__dirname, './dist'),
    filename: 'index.js'
  },
  devtool: 'cheap-module-source-map',
  module: {
    rules: [
      {
        test: /\.ts$/,
        exclude: /node_modules/,
        use: {
          loader: 'ts-loader',
        },
      }
    ],
  },
  plugins: [
    new HTMLWebpackPlugin(),
    new CleanWebpackPlugin(),
  ]
};

So, let’s explain each config option individually

mode basically allows you to set what kind of build you want, so here we are setting it to development in which case the build is formatted by adding line breaks and detailed comments making it look more readable. If you change that to production you will notice how the build gets minified and compressed.

entry obviously tells webpack its entry point, where it should start its magic on.

output helps us specify the name of the build file and where it should be located, so here we use Node’s path module to provide us a utility to work with directory paths instead of having to write it ourselves, then we call the file index.js.

___dirname is an env variable that returns the directory path of the current JS file, which is the root. _

After that, we set devtool to ​​cheap-module-source-map.
By default, devtool uses eval function in its source map styling which is a bad practice and chrome doesn't allow using it, so this would remove it.

In module we specify a set of rules and here we determine how webpack will handle Typescript files using the ts-loader.

We set a regular expression in test of what file endings to run the ts-loader on, and in exclude we are telling webpack not to traverse through the node_modules dependencies.

Unlike loaders which apply changes on files before they are bundled, plugins work on the whole generated bundle and control how they are created and in which form.

Here we are adding a couple of plugins, html-webpack-plugin generates an HTML file that includes our webpack bundle using a script tag without the need to create it manually. clean-webpack-plugin simply removes all files inside the build directory after every successful rebuild.

Okay, let's move onto the main part and start building our app

We create src directory that we specified in the webpack entry and inside of it we create index.ts which will contain all of our code.

mkdir src && cd src && touch index.ts

Copy the following code into index.ts

let appEl = document.createElement('div');
appEl.setAttribute('id', 'app')
document.body.appendChild(appEl);

let app = document.getElementById('app')!;

let balanceEl = document.createElement('h2');
balanceEl.setAttribute("id", "balance");
balanceEl.innerText = "0 ETH";
app.appendChild(balanceEl);

let inputEl = document.createElement('input');
inputEl.setAttribute('type', 'text');
inputEl.setAttribute('id', 'address-input');
inputEl.setAttribute('name', 'address-input');
inputEl.setAttribute('placeholder', "Wallet Address');
app.appendChild(inputEl);


let submitEl = document.createElement('button');
submitEl.setAttribute('id', 'submit-btn');
submitEl.innerText = 'Get Balance';
app.appendChild(submitEl);

Here we create our interface for the app, just a simple input, a button and a text element.

Notice how document.getElementById('app') is followed by an exclamation mark (!). Typescript forces us to be clearer while writing our code. It's not sure that it would return something that is not null or undefined so it throws an error, and by adding the non-null assertion operator, we are stating that the element must return something that actually makes sense.

Now we build the functionality

Web3.js

web3.js is a collection of libraries written in Nodejs, used to help us interact with Ethereum nodes.

Let's start by installing the web3 dependency

We import web3 into index.ts

const Web3 = require('web3');

Then we add the following function

const getBalance = async () => {
  submitEl.innerText = "Loading...";
  submitEl.style.pointerEvents = 'none';
  const infura = 'https://goerli.infura.io/v3/your-api-key';
  const web3 = new Web3(new Web3.providers.HttpProvider(infura));
  let addressValue = (document.getElementById('address-input') as HTMLFormElement).value;
  let balance = await web3.eth.getBalance(addressValue);
  balance = web3.utils.fromWei(balance, 'ether');
  balanceEl.innerText = balance + " ETH";
  submitEl.innerText = "Get Balance";
  submitEl.style.pointerEvents = "auto";
};

By default Typescript sets the type of an async function's return to Promise<type> because it's guaranteed that it will return a promise and since we don't typically have a return value in our function it sets the type to void.

As we mentioned earlier, Typescript forces us to be explicit, so here in addressValue we are setting document.getElementById('address-input') as HTMLFormElement just to assure that this input element has an actual value.

To connect to the Ethereum node, we pass a provider into the web3 constructor, here we are passing an HttpProvider, but you can also use WebSocketProvider which allows subscriptions and IpcProvider if the node is running locally.

We then get our network endpoint from Infura after you create an API key and pass it right into the provider.

Here we used fromWei utility to convert the balance that we get into a readable ether value.

Full code:

const Web3 = require('web3');

let appEl = document.createElement('div');
appEl.setAttribute('id', 'app')
document.body.appendChild(appEl);

let app = document.getElementById('app')!;

let balanceEl = document.createElement('h2');
balanceEl.setAttribute("id", "balance");
balanceEl.innerText = "0 ETH";
app.appendChild(balanceEl);

let inputEl = document.createElement('input');
inputEl.setAttribute("type", "text");
inputEl.setAttribute("id", "address-input");
inputEl.setAttribute("name", "address-input");
inputEl.setAttribute("placeholder", "Wallet Address");
app.appendChild(inputEl);


let submitEl = document.createElement('button');
submitEl.setAttribute("id", "submit-btn");
submitEl.innerText = "Get Balance";
app.appendChild(submitEl);

const getBalance = async () => {
  submitEl.innerText = "Loading...";
  submitEl.style.pointerEvents = 'none';
  const infura = 'https://goerli.infura.io/v3/9c29032f150b4868adf2b54794b3f0ec';
  const web3 = new Web3(new Web3.providers.HttpProvider(infura));
  let addressValue = (document.getElementById('address-input') as HTMLFormElement).value;
  let balance = await web3.eth.getBalance(addressValue);
  balance = web3.utils.fromWei(balance, 'ether');
  balanceEl.innerText = balance + " ETH";
  submitEl.innerText = "Get Balance";
  submitEl.style.pointerEvents = "auto";
};

(document.getElementById("submit-btn") as HTMLFormElement).onclick = (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  getBalance();
};

Now we move to the interesting part of how to turn all of that into a Chrome extension.

Chrome Extension and Manifest:

Manifest is the system that governs how Chrome extensions interact with your browser.

Let's create public in the root directory and inside of it we create manifest.json

mkdir public && cd public && touch manifest.json

In manifest.json we specify basic metadata and information about our extension such as the name, the description, the version that chrome currently supports, and aspects of the extension's functionality such as actions.

{
    "name": "Web3Balance",
    "description": "Get ETH Balance of any given address",
    "version": "1.0",
    "manifest_version": 3,
    "action": {
      "default_popup": "index.html"
    }
  }

We set the default popup which appears when the user clicks the extension's icon into the html file that webpack is going to generate for us.

Let's now install an additional plugin:

npm i copy-webpack-plugin --save-dev

copy-webpack-plugin as the name implies copies the manifest.json file that we have inside public right into the dist folder so that the manifest file is able to access the generated bundle.

Let’s now modify webpack.config.js and add the plugin into it

const HTMLWebpackPlugin = require('html-webpack-plugin');
const CopyWebpackPlugin = require('copy-webpack-plugin');
const { CleanWebpackPlugin } = require('clean-webpack-plugin');
const path = require('path');

module.exports = {
  mode: 'development',
  entry: "./src/index.ts",
  output: {
    path: path.resolve(__dirname, './dist'),
    filename: 'index.js'
  },
  devtool: "​​cheap-module-source-map",
  module: {
    rules: [
      {
        test: /\.ts$/,
        exclude: /node_modules/,
        use: {
          loader: "ts-loader",
        },
      }
    ],
  },
  plugins: [
    new HTMLWebpackPlugin(),
    new CopyWebpackPlugin({
      patterns: [
        {from: "public", to: "."}
      ],
    }),
    new CleanWebpackPlugin(),
  ]
};

Now we want to add some styling to our app and for that we will need a couple of loaders

npm i css-loader style-loader --save-dev

css-loader simply collects all of our project's css files helping webpack to resolve them, and style-loader takes the generated output and put it inside the style tags.

Let's modify webpack.config.js

const HTMLWebpackPlugin = require('html-webpack-plugin');
const CopyWebpackPlugin = require('copy-webpack-plugin');
const { CleanWebpackPlugin } = require('clean-webpack-plugin');
const path = require('path');

module.exports = {
  mode: 'development',
  entry: "./src/index.ts",
  output: {
    path: path.resolve(__dirname, './dist'),
    filename: 'index.js'
  },
  devtool: "​​cheap-module-source-map",
  module: {
    rules: [
      {
        test: /\.ts$/,
        exclude: /node_modules/,
        use: {
          loader: "ts-loader",
        },
      },
      {
        test: /\.css$/,
        exclude: /node_modules/,
        use: ["style-loader", "css-loader"],
      }
    ],
  },
  plugins: [
    new HTMLWebpackPlugin(),
    new CopyWebpackPlugin({
      patterns: [
        {from: "public", to: "."}
      ],
    }),
    new CleanWebpackPlugin(),
  ]
};

Create style.css in the src directory and copy the following code into it

#app{
    width: 350px;
    padding: 20px;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    justify-content: center;
    align-items: center;
}

#balance{
    margin: 0;
}

#address-input{
    width: 100%;
    height: 30px;
    padding: 6px 15px;
    margin: 20px 0;
    border: none;
    outline: none;
    border-radius: 25px;
    background-color: #f3f3f3;
}

#submit-btn{
    width: 50%;
    padding: 8px;
    cursor: pointer;
    border: none;
    border-radius: 8px;
    outline: none;
}

Now we import the file into index.ts

Finally, let's add the build script into package.json

"scripts": {
    "build": "webpack"
}

Now we run the following command in our terminal

After the build is done, you will find the dist folder created in the root directory containing the generated bundle.

Let's now head to the Chrome browser and navigate to chrome://extensions

On the top left of the page click on Load unpacked to fetch the dist directory

Et voila!

Full code can be found in the github repo here.

Conclusion:

Congrats! You just created your first ever Web3 Chrome Extension! With Typescript you may put some more effort writing your code, however I believe the effort pays off and the benefits are worth it.

I'm planning to build more complex projects that cover a lot of in-depth Web3 topics, so keep posted!
item.author: thisisgazzar
